Add chunked date-range retry for large repos (502 fallback) (#73)

* Add chunked date-range retry for large repos (502 fallback)

When the full 90-day merged-PR query returns persistent HTTP 502 (as
happens with high-volume repos like dotnet/dotnet-api-docs), the second
pass now splits the date range into 30-day chunks and queries each
independently.

Changes:
- Add optional EndDate parameter to Invoke-RepoMaintainerScan to
  support merged:A..B date-range queries
- Add Invoke-ChunkedRepoScan function that computes non-overlapping
  chunks, scans each, and merges results (MergerCounts summed,
  Activity paths/labels concatenated)
- Replace second-pass same-query retry with chunked scan
- Add ChunkDays validation (1-365)
- Add 11 Pester tests for chunk computation, result merging, partial
  failure handling, and EndDate parameter passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: 修复 Xray 下载文件名与实际 release 不匹配导致安装失败

根本原因：
- check_arch() 返回 amd64/arm64，但 Xray release 实际文件名为
  Xray-linux-64.zip 和 Xray-linux-arm64-v8a.zip
- curl 缺少 --fail 参数，404 页面被当作正常下载
- 子 shell 中 || true 吞掉所有错误，安装失败但仍提示成功

修复内容：
- check_arch() 改为返回 64 / arm64-v8a 匹配 release 命名
- 所有 curl 下载改用 -fSL，HTTP 错误立即失败

- update_geodata 末尾增加文件存在性验证

docs: add ADR-0001 system architecture (accepted)

ADR-0001 defines the foundational architecture for Naked
Time:

- Background daemon (timed) with user-scoped lifecycle
  via launchd, systemd, and Windows Task Scheduler
- Unix domain sockets for IPC on all platforms including
  Windows 11
- gRPC as the primary API protocol with protobuf contracts
- SQLite in WAL mode for local data storage
- TOML configuration
- Plugin architecture with child process supervision and
  per-plugin UDS
- Python AI agent via LangChain Deep Agents SDK
- Separate MCP server binary (time-mcp)
- Go as the implementation language for all core components
  (timed, time-cli, time-mcp)
- Cross-compilation targeting 9 platforms via GOOS/GOARCH

* fix(copilot-cli): remove conflicting gh alias before extension install (#63)

built-in command or alias' when a prior partial install left a gh
alias named copilot.

before attempting the extension install.

Also removed the gh copilot --version post-install check, which

* fix(copilot-cli): handle gh built-in promotion in 2.x+

gh 2.89.0 promotes 'gh copilot' to a built-in command. The previous
fix checked gh alias list, which doesn't catch built-ins.

Two-part fix:
1. Idempotency check now uses 'gh copilot --help' — works for
   built-ins, extensions, and aliases.
2. Wrap install attempt with set +e to capture the "matches built-in"
   error and exit gracefully instead of failing the whole setup.

Alias guard retained as defensive code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(squad): log gh built-in promotion learning to donald history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(copilot-cli): use 'gh copilot -- --help' for idempotency check

On gh 2.89.0+, 'gh copilot --help' exits 0 even before the Copilot
CLI binary has been downloaded — it shows gh's own wrapper help and
never touches the binary. This caused the idempotency check to always
short-circuit to 'already available' on a fresh system.

Fix: use 'gh copilot -- --help' instead. The '--' passes the flag
through to the actual Copilot CLI binary, so:
  - binary already present → exits 0 immediately (idempotent)
  - binary not yet downloaded → triggers proactive download, then exits 0
  - older gh without extension → exits non-zero → falls through to install

Also update the file-header comment to document this nuance.

Add onCreateCommand to strip carriage returns from all shell scripts
before postCreateCommand runs. This guards against the case where a
Windows user's working tree still has CRLF line endings after the
PR #66 gitattributes fix, which updates git's index but NOT the

- Safe on Linux: no-op if files are already LF
